missed_steps: The student did not normalize the archive path and extraction path before the first extraction attempt.
The student did not perform a focused PE triage step after locating the extracted executable.
The student did not verify any candidate flag with a controlled rerun or logic reconstruction.
bad_tool_choices: Large unrelated workspace searches happened before engaging directly with the provided challenge path.
The student attempted 7z without checking whether the binary existed in the environment.
The student used broad string scraping that produced massive low-signal output instead of escalating to a decompiler/disassembler.
bad_recovery_points: After the first path failure, recovery was slow and included multiple low-confidence extraction attempts.
After seeing truncated string output, the student continued heuristic guessing instead of narrowing scope or changing tools.
evidence_gaps: No exact extracted executable path was stabilized in the notes.
No validation trace tied the proposed fragments to a final flag.

next_actions.note: Make non-ASCII Windows path normalization mandatory before extraction or file reads.
Teach explicit Windows fallback ordering: Expand-Archive -> .NET ZipFile -> external archiver only if available.
After one executable is confirmed, require focused PE triage before any large raw string dump.
Keep this case in train/dev only; prepare a separate unseen reverse sample for held-out eval.
